name: Corn Stuffed Poblano Chiles
recipeInstructions:
1. Set the oven to broil.
2. Cut the chiles lengthwise into halves and carefully remove all the seeds.
3. Place the cut sides down on the broiler rack about 4 inches from the heat source. Broil until the skin blisters on the peppers for about 15 minutes.
4. Remove the chiles from the oven and let them cool in a plastic bag for 15 minutes.
5. Carefully remove as much of the blistered skin as possible.
6. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
7. In a medium bowl, beat the eggs until thick and lemon-colored, about 4 minutes.
8. Stir in the remaining ingredients.
9. Place the chiles in a greased rectangular baking dish, 13 x 13 x 2 inches.
10. Spoon about 1/4 cup of the corn mixture into each chile half.
11. Cover and bake until the corn mixture is hot, about 25 minutes.
